confirmed.note: MergeDim+RequireRank4 helpers and the Q/K/V rank-4/MergeDim attention skeleton copied verbatim (only error-message op-name string changed) from shape_preview.cc ComputeShapeFlexAttention (f3f220a); local helpers re-declared per file instead of shared

MergeDim helper body identical to the one in shape_attention.cc / shape_preview.cc, only the error-message prefix (ComputeShapeGridSample) and the param name (axis_name) differ; same anon-namespace helper copied a third time rather than factored into a shared shapes/ header

consecutive TEST(BackendTestCase, <Op>...) blocks are ~20-line near-identical clones (collect cases -> find by name -> assert sizes -> loop EXPECT_NEAR), differing only in op name literal and the std:: math function; e.g. Acos vs Acosh diverged-near copy

false_positives.what_happened: shape_mul.cc and shape_sub.cc are 24-line per-operator dispatch wrappers that differ only in the op-name literal ("Mul" vs "Sub") and the matching comment word; a near-100%% cross-file match. The real logic is already deduplicated into the shared ComputeShapeBinaryBroadcast() helper they both call -- these files are thin registration shims, the project's deliberate one-file-per-op convention, not duplicated logic.
false_positives.fix_proposal: #056: add a 'thin-wrapper' suppressor -- if a flagged cross-file clone is a single function whose body is <=3 statements and >=1 of them is a call to a shared symbol that ALSO appears in the other clone (same callee), drop it. More generally raise the cross-file minimum-token floor (>= ~25 non-comment tokens of UNIQUE, non-call body) so single-line-delegation files stop matching; tie-break with 'distinct identifiers / total identifiers' ratio -- when the only difference between two files is one string/enum literal AND the rest is a call to a common helper, classify as boilerplate-dispatch, not clone.

cycle.desc: no cyclic SCC; archcheck --graph reports sccs_cyclic=0, largest_scc=1 over 53 nodes / 85 edges
cycle.evidence: archcheck --graph on /home/localadm/oss/_aidev_dense/xadupre_onnx-light: nodes=53 edges=85 sccs_cyclic=0 largest_scc=1; include graph is a DAG (note: 303 external + 48 unresolved includes, so vendored ONNX subtrees are largely outside the resolved author graph)
